items.title: Anthropic pauses token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has temporarily paused token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK during the beta phase, meaning you can build and run multi-agent workflows without worrying about racking up massive API bills. This is a massive opportunity to experiment with complex agent behaviors for free. Try this week: Write a 10-line script using the Claude Agent SDK to automate fetching and filtering your daily AI-news candidates for AI-ingest without spending a dime.

items.title: 15-year-old builds and open-sources Weave, an AI screen-action assistant
items.why_it_matters: A 15-year-old developer has shipped Weave, an open-source AI assistant that watches your screen and acts before you ask. It is direct proof that age and formal credentials do not matter when it comes to shipping real software. Try this week: Clone the Weave repository, look at how they handle local screen capture, and see if you can use a similar approach to capture your browser state.

items.title: Kevin: A Claude Code plugin that cuts token costs by 90%
items.why_it_matters: Kevin is a new open-source Claude Code plugin that forces Claude to write highly concise code and speak less, saving up to 90% on token costs and preventing you from hitting rate limits. This directly optimizes your daily coding workflow. Try this week: Run `/plugin marketplace add hvardhan878/kevin` in your Claude Code terminal today to immediately cut down your token spend while building AI-ingest.

items.title: Wattfare: Let users bring their own AI budget to your web app
items.why_it_matters: Wattfare is a new service that lets users bring their own AI budget to your web app via an OAuth-like button, solving the problem of developers getting hit with massive API bills for free users. This makes shipping public AI tools financially risk-free. Try this week: Sign up for Wattfare and integrate their SDK into your Next.js project to let users authenticate with their own OpenAI or Anthropic keys.

items.title: git-lrc: Free, micro AI code reviews that run on git commit
items.why_it_matters: This tool runs lightweight, free AI code reviews locally right when you run `git commit`, helping you catch bugs and understand your code before it even reaches a PR. It is perfect for reinforcing your commit discipline. Try this week: Install `git-lrc` in your AI-ingest repository and run your next commit with it to see how it critiques your code while it's still fresh in your mind.

items.title: Migrating from Claude to DeepSeek without breaking everything
items.why_it_matters: This guide walks through the practical steps of swapping Claude for DeepSeek in your codebase without breaking your existing prompts or structured outputs, allowing you to save massive amounts on API costs. Try this week: Change the API base URL in your AI-ingest backend from Anthropic to DeepSeek's endpoint for your basic text-filtering tasks to see if you can cut costs by 90%.

items.title: AgentPace: Track your Claude Code and Codex usage from your macOS menu bar
items.why_it_matters: AgentPace is a macOS menu bar app that tracks your Claude Code and Codex usage trends over time, helping you visualize your burn rate so you don't unexpectedly run out of usage limits. Try this week: Download the app and keep it open during your next coding session to see exactly how many tokens your AI-ingest builds are consuming.
